mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in INDPRO signals improving production momentum and near-term earnings revisions, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, in walk-forward tests, positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns less often than the unconditional base rate for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, labor-market deterioration signals falling household income and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest vintage for 2011-05 was 9.1 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below unconditional mean return at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises faster than its trailing 12-month pace while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, inflation pressure with a policy-rate floor erodes real income and raises future tightening risk, causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest vintage for 2011-05 was 0.09 percent
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% has nonnegative sign or Rank IC below 0.03 in 20-day walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in RSAFS signals stronger consumer demand and revenue growth for listed firms, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest vintage for 2011-05 was 387117 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes are not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month RSAFS changes over at least 70% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below 600 thousand annualized units, construction weakness signals impaired credit creation and household balance-sheet stress, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest vintage for 2011-04 was 523 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 60-day forward returns after HOUST below 600 are not lower than returns after HOUST above 600, or if the threshold coefficient is positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS grows over 3 months while UNRATE does not fall, payroll gains are not absorbing slack and earnings-quality concerns dominate headline job growth, causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest vintage for 2011-05 was 131043 thousand persons
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-nonfalling condition does not underperform the PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-falling condition in 20-day returns by at least 25 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 posts a positive quarter-over-quarter change and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, real growth with accommodative policy raises expected cash flows without raising discount rates, causing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest vintage for 2011-01 was 13441.9 billions of chained dollars
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth under FEDFUNDS below 0.25% is followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean in more than half of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in FEDFUNDS signals a tightening surprise that raises discount rates and reduces equity duration value, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest vintage for 2011-05 was 0.09 percent
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS monthly increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 65% of cases or if the 5-day return coefficient on FEDFUNDS change is nonnegative.
